rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (3/10) and rest on identical job-relevant reasoning: strong TS/Node/DLang breadth, some C/C++ IDE work at National Instruments, but no Kotlin, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate, and only dated/marginal Java with no JVM depth. The variant is a reworded version of the same substance with no new concern, penalty, or shifted reasoning tied to the name change.
